last_completed_item: D-218 — Modular room packages assembled through a lightweight authored level graph
current_item: D-219 — Canonical mission and room runtime-state ownership
next_action: Ask exactly one concise A/B/C decision card selecting where authoritative mission and room runtime state lives: primarily inside loaded Unity scene objects; in one centralized mutable mission manager with rooms reaching into it directly; or in a typed engine-independent mission-state model keyed by stable IDs, with rooms acting as projections and submitting validated state transitions through explicit commands or events. Preserve modular room packages, lightweight level graph assembly, permanent cleared-room and route state, checkpoint rollback groups, dedicated banking, fast travel, suspend snapshots, versioned local saves, additive loading, automated validation, future co-op coarse canonical state, and plain-C# domain logic. Include death rollback, scene unload and reload, save migration, debugging, testability, accidental direct mutation, performance, authoring ergonomics, transactional reward safety, and risk of overengineering. Put the recommendation after all A/B/C options at the end of the card. D-211 through D-218 are checkpointed as queued but not formally batch-persisted; ask D-219 and D-220, then persist the complete D-211 through D-220 batch unless the user changes the cadence or requests a handoff.
resume_instruction: Read assembly/context/NEW_CHAT_RESUME.md first and follow it exactly.
blockers: Recovered decisions D-001 through D-039 require re-verification before final requirements
The product repository uses a bootstrap kit installed before the latest handoff framework additions; current resume files were added directly for this test

The authoritative Intake Interviewer prompt path assembly/prompts/00-intake-interviewer.md is currently missing from this branch and should be restored from the framework before final intake generation
The D-101 through D-210 decisions are persisted as authoritative batch-extension artifacts and should be consolidated into the primary live log and session snapshot during a later maintenance pass

persistence.batching_rule: Checkpoint queued state after several questions for flow; formally persist D-211 through D-220 at D-220 unless an earlier handoff is requested

constraints: The complete factory slice must provide the reliable end-to-end loop including saves recovery checkpoints banking loot risk shop strongboxes difficulty completion replay performance and diagnostics
Before passing the gate add at least one new room or encounter one new enemy and one new weapon through the intended repeatable production pipelines
Those additions must not require foundational-system rewrites
Critical systems require automated smoke and regression coverage save interruption tests and representative performance budgets
Content ownership boundaries must support isolated parallel human or AI-agent branches
Final environment polish complete music cinematics all difficulty tuning and the mature economy may remain deferred
Most Stage 2 content may remain coherent temporary art while a representative subset passes through the intended real production pipeline
The subset includes the player mech at least one ordinary enemy the elite or upgraded droid at least two visually different weapons and one major factory machine or environment set
Prove representative shadows normal maps emissive effects damage feedback destruction states animation and dimensional 2D presentation
Document import settings pivots scale sorting collision animation memory and performance requirements
Another artist or AI-assisted workflow must be able to add equivalent content without repairing every asset manually inside Unity
Final art for the remaining factory content stays deferred until the pipeline is validated
The primary developer first produces one reference example and documents files folders data definitions validation naming imports tests previews save implications and common failures
An isolated AI-agent branch or occasional collaborator must then add another small representative weapon enemy variant or room encounter without direct foundational implementation help
The contribution must integrate through review and automated checks without modifying unrelated core systems
Missing documentation discovered during reproduction must be corrected
Merge conflicts ownership boundaries and repeatability must be evaluated rather than manually rescuing one special contribution

Every content contribution first passes automated checks for required fields valid ranges stable identifiers references import rules loot and progression eligibility save compatibility and basic runtime behavior
Automated smoke tests should cover representative spawn fire damage death completion and performance warnings where practical
Passing automation means safe to review rather than approved for the game
Playable review must judge combat feel readability audiovisual feedback encounter purpose weapon identity four-weapon interaction collision navigation and accessibility behavior
Human and AI-agent contributions use the same validation gates
Ordinary weapons enemies rooms encounters objectives hazards and rewards are assembled from typed ScriptableObject definitions and reusable behavior modules
Provide a compact reusable module set for representative firing movement enemy objective hazard and reward archetypes
Genuinely novel mechanics may add isolated reusable code extensions rather than one-item hacks
New extensions require automated tests playable validation documentation and compatibility with saves loot diagnostics and four-weapon interactions
Ordinary content additions must not modify unrelated foundational systems or force all creativity through a single bloated universal schema
Typed ScriptableObject assets remain the canonical runtime and ordinary authoring format
Every item uses a stable human-readable content identifier and participates in automated searchable registries
Generate deterministic text summaries or snapshots so content changes are reviewable outside opaque Unity asset diffs
Use CSV or JSON import selectively for bulk balance tables localization loot weights or highly repetitive definitions
Generated files are never hand-edited and import export round trips must be deterministic and tested
Explicit ownership boundaries reduce parallel edits to the same Unity assets
Each weapon enemy encounter or room contribution lives mainly inside an owned feature folder containing definitions prefabs effects audio art tests previews and documentation
Shared registries lookup tables and build indexes are generated or updated through deterministic tooling rather than manually edited by every contributor
Central balance tables and foundational systems have explicit owners and require separate justified tasks
Contributions should be small reviewable removable and rollback-safe with minimal Unity YAML conflicts
Automated validation must reject duplicate identifiers missing references and incompatible package content before integration
Human and AI-agent branches follow the same ownership and merge boundaries
Each meaningful room or encounter is an independently previewable and testable prefab or additive scene package

Room contracts cover stable identity doorway connections encounter lifecycle enemy and pickup ownership persistent state lighting sorting collision navigation teleports shops banking rewards and performance budgets
A lightweight authored level graph defines room connections and controls mission-wide structure and state
Small corridors and decorative connectors may remain lightweight prefabs rather than requiring a full scene each
Cross-room communication uses stable identifiers and explicit events instead of fragile direct scene-object references
The structure must support independent branch ownership future streaming and later co-op room instances without overengineering the Stage 2 slice
